title: allium sativum
intro: Allium sativum, commonly known as garlic, is a perennial plant belonging to the Allium genus, characterized by its distinctive odor and flavor. The medicinal properties of Allium sativum are attributed to its sulfur compounds, which have antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of consuming Allium sativum include lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and acting as an antioxidant, in addition to its ability to boost the immune system and aid in digestion. Garlic preparations include raw, cooked, or powdered forms, as well as supplements, and is often used in traditional medicine and culinary dishes worldwide.
